usefulness_evaluation.narrative: This is a structural/adversarial test — usefulness scores are not applicable. All 10 edge-case test vectors passed: validation interceptor correctly catches malformed UUIDs, empty/oversized strings, and invalid status transitions. Delete on non-existent booking is idempotent (returns empty success). ListTrips on empty collection returns valid empty response. Error codes are precise (invalid_argument vs not_found vs failed_precondition) with descriptive messages.

test_results.actual: invalid_argument: validation error: id: value must be a valid UUID
invalid_argument: validation error: title: value length must be at least 1 characters
invalid_argument: validation error: title: value length must be at most 512 characters
failed_precondition: invalid trip status transition: completed -> planning
test_results.notes: State machine is enforced. PLANNING->ACTIVE->COMPLETED is the valid forward path. Backward transitions are rejected with failed_precondition error code, which is the correct gRPC code for this scenario.

test_results.actual: invalid_argument: validation error: content: value length must be at least 1 characters
invalid_argument: validation error: content: value length must be at most 10000 characters

test_results.notes: Delete is idempotent — returns empty success for non-existent IDs. This is a valid design choice.

test_results.actual: invalid_argument: validation error: raw_text: value length must be at least 1 characters
Success with pagination:{} and no trips array (empty collection)
test_results.notes: Returns pagination object with no trips field rather than an empty array. This is standard protobuf behavior — empty repeated fields are omitted from JSON serialization.
